text: A scientifically based in-race nutritional strategy improves marathon finish time by approximately 4.7% (about 11 minutes) compared to a freely chosen strategy in recreational runners.
A target in-race intake of approximately 0.750 L water, 60 g maltodextrin and glucose, 0.06 g sodium, and 0.09 g caffeine per hour is an effective marathon fueling strategy.
specific_value: ~750 mL water + 60 g carbs + 0.06 g sodium + 0.09 g caffeine per hour

text: A structured in-race carbohydrate intake rate of 60 g per hour from maltodextrin and glucose supports improved marathon performance.
Following a scientifically based in-race nutrition plan does not increase gastrointestinal symptoms compared to a freely chosen intake strategy during a marathon.
Caffeine intake of approximately 0.09 g per hour during a marathon, as part of a combined nutrition strategy, contributes to improved performance without increasing gastrointestinal distress.
